verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is predominantly ER-resident, but exhibits a documented, functional plasma membrane pool with its N-terminal domain accessible from the extracellular face. The protein reaches the PM via its own type I TM anchor. Surface-applied antibodies can engage the extracellular N-terminus and modulate SOCE. The PM pool is minor but physiologically real and functionally relevant. This constitutes dual localization with documented surface accessibility.
